summary: AFI's normative protocol surface is owned almost entirely by afi-config (the self-declared 'single source of truth'): the USS v1/v1.1 and CPJ v0.1 ingest dialects, vault/pipeline/blueprint/plugin-manifest/validatorConfig schemas, and the REGISTRIES_AND_REPUTATION.v0.1 invariants doc; the canonical evidence record (VaultedSignalRecord in afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts), the VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1 invariants (bundled inside the reference repo afi-reactor), and the on-chain mint/receipt semantics in afi-token/src complete the law. The lifecycle RAW->ENRICHED->ANALYZED->SCORED->MINTED->REPLAYED is a normative concept but is NOT machine-enforceable from the canonical schema library (vault.schema.json exposes only a boolean stageIndex hint, no enumerated stage field), and most stages are defined-but-unwritten in code (per Theme D). Several normative invariants are stated only in prose and unenforced by code: vault immutability/finality (the only canonical writer overwrites via deleteOne+insertOne), determinism/replay (no pinned topology/version/content-hash on the record; Math.random execution IDs; reactor scores land in a divergent store), and reputation-must-not-override-scoring (honor-system doc with no code guard). The ingest boundary is split and inconsistent: the reactor's own server enforces USS v1.1/CPJ v0.1 via AJV (with a deterministic ingestHash), but the production afi-gateway ingest route accepts a non-USS VaultedSignalRecord shape and writes straight to the vault, so the canonical dialect is bypassed on the tenant path.

answers.question: A1: Which schemas/types/docs are normative (protocol law) vs reference implementation?
answers.answer: The normative surface is owned primarily by afi-config, which self-identifies as protocol law: AFI_CONFIG_OVERVIEW.md states 'schemas and invariants here are normative (protocol law)' while afi-reactor/afi-infra/Mongo adapters are 'reference implementations - demonstration spines, not mandatory stacks' (afi-config/docs/AFI_CONFIG_OVERVIEW.md:13-22). The concrete normative artifacts are: the USS ingest dialect (schemas/usignal/v1_1/index.schema.json + core.schema.json, plus legacy v1/) and its four lens schemas (schemas/usignal/v1/lenses/{equity,strategy,macro,onchain}.lens.schema.json); the CPJ v0.1 dialect (schemas/cpj/v0_1/index.schema.json + core.schema.json); and the configuration contracts vault.schema.json, pipeline.schema.json, blueprint.schema.json, plugin-manifest.schema.json, validatorConfig.schema.json, analyst-config.schema.json and definitions/enrichment-node.schema.json. The governance/determinism invariants are doc-normative in afi-config/docs/REGISTRIES_AND_REPUTATION.v0.1.md. Beyond afi-config, the canonical EVIDENCE record shape is normative in afi-infra (VaultedSignalRecord + stage snapshots at afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts:331,10-16, spec afi-infra/docs/TSSD_VAULT_SPEC.md); the REPLAY invariants are declared normative in afi-reactor/docs/VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md:67; and the on-chain commitment semantics are normative in afi-token/src/*.sol (AFIMintCoordinator.MintCoordinated event afi-token/src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:38-44). This matches the portable-spec normative table (afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:4.1). Two caveats blur the law/reference line: (a) the generic afi-config schemas hard-label themselves to specific org repos ('...in afi-reactor', '...in afi-infra'), and (b) the canonical library contains NO Commitment-plane schema and NO Market/analytics-plane schema, so two of the five protocol planes are unrepresented in the source of truth.

answers.question: A2: Which invariants are stated (in docs/specs) but NOT enforced in code?
answers.answer: At least five normative invariants are prose-only with no runtime enforcement. (1) VAULT IMMUTABILITY/FINALITY: REGISTRIES_AND_REPUTATION.v0.1.md:111 'Vault finality (T.S.S.D. Vault immutability) is absolute; reputation cannot rewrite history', but the only canonical writer mutates records - MongoTSSDVaultClient.upsert sets updatedAt=now and, when a record exists, does deleteOne(filter) then insertOne(doc) (afi-infra/src/tssd/MongoTSSDVaultClient.ts:148-159), i.e. overwrite-in-place; nothing prevents re-writing a finalized signal. (2) DETERMINISM/REPLAY: VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md:37 'Same codex version + same config + same data snapshot -> same validator outputs', but VaultedSignalRecord pins no codex/DAG-topology/plugin/scorer version or content hash (afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts:331-362; only optional analystScore.strategyVersion at types.ts:112), and the DAG executor generates execution IDs with Math.random (afi-reactor/src/dag/DAGExecutor.ts:1002-1003) - so 'same inputs' is not bindable to a stored record. (3) REPUTATION NON-INTERFERENCE: REGISTRIES_AND_REPUTATION.v0.1.md:105 'Reputation cannot alter the Unified Weighting Rule (UWR)' and :115 validators 'remain dumb, deterministic' are entirely honor-system - there is no code guard; this is an invariant 'Derived from AFI Protocol white paper' (REGISTRIES_AND_REPUTATION.v0.1.md:205) with no schema or assertion enforcing it. (4) PROVENANCE/TIMESTAMP: AFI_CONFIG_OVERVIEW.md:122 'All signals must include provenance.timestamp', yet USS v1.1 demotes timestamp to a 'legacy field' and does NOT require it (afi-config/schemas/usignal/v1_1/index.schema.json:235-239 require source/providerId/signalId only). (5) QUALITY: the overview openly states 'Nothing is network-enforced at the schema level' (AFI_CONFIG_OVERVIEW.md:121), confirming the schema layer is advisory. The replay ReplaySession invariants are also only 'SHOULD capture' (VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md ValidatorReplaySession section).

answers.question: A3: What is the complete ingest boundary (USS, CPJ, lenses, webhooks) - defined vs enforced?
answers.answer: DEFINED (normative): the ingest dialect is USS v1.1 (afi-config/schemas/usignal/v1_1/index.schema.json - required 'schema' const 'afi.usignal.v1.1' + provenance{source,providerId,signalId}, with a replay-canonical 'facts' block at index.schema.json:31 and an ingestHash field at :258) and, upstream of it, CPJ v0.1 as 'the first normalization stage before USS v1.1 mapping' (afi-config/schemas/cpj/v0_1/index.schema.json:5). Four lenses (equity/strategy/macro/onchain) are declared, but the 'lens' field is OPTIONAL and intake 'will auto-detect or treat as generic' (afi-config/schemas/usignal/v1_1/index.schema.json:14-30), so lens/payload coherence is not schema-enforced. ENFORCED (where): the reactor's own ingest server enforces the dialect via AJV - POST /api/webhooks/tradingview maps TradingView->USS and runs validateUsignalV11 (afi-reactor/src/server.ts:207-219), and POST /api/ingest/cpj validates CPJ, maps CPJ->USS, re-validates USS, and computes a deterministic SHA256 ingestHash with key-sorting + array canonicalization (afi-reactor/src/server.ts:308-360; afi-reactor/src/uss/cpjMapper.ts:220-256); validators load the canonical schemas from afi-config (afi-reactor/src/uss/ussValidator.ts:79-94). BYPASSED (the gap): the production afi-gateway tenant ingest, POST /api/v1/signals, does NOT validate USS/CPJ at all - normalizeSignalPayload only requires a VaultedSignalRecord identity{signalId,epochId,market,timeframe} and writes the record straight to the vault (afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:20-58,135). So there are two divergent ingest contracts: a USS/CPJ-conformant reactor webhook path and a non-USS gateway path that skips the canonical dialect entirely.

answers.question: A4: Is the canonical lifecycle (RAW->ENRICHED->ANALYZED->SCORED->MINTED->REPLAYED) defined vs implemented per stage?
answers.answer: DEFINED: the six-stage lifecycle is enumerated as a TypeScript union SignalLifecycleStage = RAW|ENRICHED|ANALYZED|SCORED|MINTED|REPLAYED with a per-stage snapshot map on VaultedSignalRecord (afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts:10-16,336-351) and is the spine of the North Star (afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:5). CRITICAL GAP IN THE NORMATIVE LIBRARY: the canonical afi-config schemas do NOT define an enumerated 'stage' field or any transition rule - vault.schema.json mentions stages only as a boolean index hint ('stageIndex ... Create index on signal stage (RAW, ENRICHED, etc.)', afi-config/schemas/vault.schema.json:93-98), so an external stack cannot machine-validate lifecycle conformance from the source of truth; the only typed lifecycle lives in afi-infra (a reference repo). IMPLEMENTED PER STAGE (confirmed by Theme D): RAW is written only by the gateway ingest, and only in observed/typical usage (the handler persists caller-supplied payload.stages ?? {}, so it is not code-enforced as RAW-only) (afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:33-58); ENRICHED and ANALYZED snapshot types exist but no code populates them; SCORED is computed and submitted as a canonical afi.scored-signal-evidence.v1 record through the afi-infra evidence store (lifecycleState SCORED at afi-reactor/src/evidence/reactorEvidenceRecord.ts:36; submitted at afi-reactor/src/evidence/submitScoredSignalEvidence.ts:196), a record separate from VaultedSignalRecord.stages.scored, which no code populates (the Reactor-owned divergent scored-signal writer was removed in afi-reactor ce4c178, PR #42); MINTED is on-chain-verifiable but no code writes the minted stage back to the canonical record; REPLAYED (OutcomeSnapshot) is never populated - the replay runner is a v0.1 read-only stub (afi-infra/src/tssd/TSSDReplayRunner.ts:40-103). Net: the lifecycle is fully DEFINED (in afi-infra types/North Star) but only partially IMPLEMENTED (RAW observed + SCORED in a parallel store), and it is NOT enforceable from the canonical schema library.

answers.question: A5: What are the determinism/replay rules (VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC, codex pins, config snapshots) and how complete are they?
answers.answer: The normative replay rules live in afi-reactor/docs/VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md, which declares itself 'normative for invariants (determinism, isolation, traceability) and concepts (ReplaySession, replayable lifecycle)' (VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md:67) and states the three invariants: Determinism ('Same codex version + same config + same data snapshot -> same validator outputs'), Isolation ('Replay must not mutate production state'), and Traceability (VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md:35-41). A ValidatorReplaySession 'SHOULD capture' codexVersion, configSnapshotId, dataSnapshotRef and timestamps, and 'Pinned inputs (codex + config + data) are required so the same replay session yields identical outputs later' (VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md ValidatorReplaySession + 'Pinned inputs' lines). GAPS: (a) the spec is normative-grade but is bundled inside the reference repo afi-reactor rather than promoted into the protocol spec set, and ReplaySession is only conceptual ('SHOULD', no JSON Schema). (b) CODEX PINS / CONFIG SNAPSHOTS are coarse: .afi-codex.json files pin repo-level module/version metadata (afi-config/.afi-codex.json codexVersion '2.0.0') but no per-signal transform version; validatorConfig.schema.json pins determinism-relevant thresholds as required (decayCheckIntervalMs, challengeWindowDurationHours, mintApprovalThreshold default 0.66, snapshotSpaceId) (afi-config/schemas/validatorConfig.schema.json:6-46), yet these config values are never snapshotted INTO a VaultedSignalRecord, so a stored signal cannot be bound to the exact thresholds/codex used. (c) POSITIVE artifact: the CPJ ingest hash is a genuine deterministic canonicalization (sorted keys + canonicalized TP/SL arrays + SHA256) (afi-reactor/src/uss/cpjMapper.ts:220-256). Net: determinism/replay is stated normatively but record-level pinning is absent and the replay engine is a stub, so deterministic re-execution is design-only (consistent with Theme D's replay-readiness finding).

findings.title: Canonical lifecycle (RAW->...->REPLAYED) is normative but has no machine-enforceable stage field in the afi-config schema library
findings.evidence: afi-config/schemas/vault.schema.json:93-98 stageIndex is only a boolean index hint 'Create index on signal stage (RAW, ENRICHED, etc.)'; the only enumerated lifecycle is in a reference repo at afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts:10-16 'RAW|ENRICHED|ANALYZED|SCORED|MINTED|REPLAYED'
findings.recommendation: Add a normative enumerated 'stage' field (and optional transition rules) to the canonical schema library (vault/signal schema) so the RAW->REPLAYED lifecycle is engine-agnostic and externally machine-checkable; carry into AFI_NORMATIVE_REGISTER.md and AFI_REPLAY_READINESS_MATRIX.md.

findings.title: Production gateway ingest bypasses the canonical USS/CPJ dialect (two divergent ingest contracts)
findings.evidence: afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:20-58 normalizeSignalPayload requires only a VaultedSignalRecord identity{signalId,epochId,market,timeframe} and never validates USS/CPJ; afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:135 'await vault.upsert(parsed.record)'. Contrast the reactor server which DOES enforce the dialect: afi-reactor/src/server.ts:211 validateUsignalV11(canonicalUss)
findings.recommendation: Route the gateway tenant ingest through USS v1.1 (and CPJ for parsed sources) validation before persistence, or document that /api/v1/signals accepts pre-canonicalized vault records only; otherwise the normative ingest dialect is unenforced on the production path. Cross-link Theme D (gateway is the only canonical writer).

findings.title: Stated vault immutability/finality invariant is unenforced - the only canonical writer overwrites records
findings.evidence: afi-config/docs/REGISTRIES_AND_REPUTATION.v0.1.md:111 'Vault finality (T.S.S.D. Vault immutability) is absolute; reputation cannot rewrite history' vs afi-infra/src/tssd/MongoTSSDVaultClient.ts:148-159 upsert sets updatedAt=now then, if a doc exists, deleteOne(filter)+insertOne(doc) (overwrite-in-place)
findings.recommendation: Enforce append-only/immutability at the canonical writer (reject re-writes of finalized signals, or version every write) so the stated finality invariant is backed by code; feed into AFI_REPLAY_READINESS_MATRIX.md and the contradiction register.

findings.title: Determinism/replay invariant is unenforced at the record level: VaultedSignalRecord pins no codex/topology/version/content-hash
findings.evidence: afi-reactor/docs/VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md:37 'Same codex version + same config + same data snapshot -> same validator outputs' vs afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts:331-362 (no dag-topology/plugin/scorer/codex version or contentHash; only optional strategyVersion at afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts:112); afi-reactor/src/dag/DAGExecutor.ts:1002-1003 generateExecutionId uses Date.now()+Math.random()
findings.recommendation: Make the replay-pinning normative: require record-level codexVersion/configSnapshotId/dagTopologyHash/payloadHash on the canonical signal record and a deterministic execution-id/ordering in the reference DAG. Shared with Theme D finding #0; promote to AFI_NORMATIVE_REGISTER.md.

findings.title: Canonical schema library omits the Commitment plane and the Market/analytics plane (2 of 5 protocol planes unrepresented)
findings.evidence: afi-config/schemas/ contains vault/pipeline/blueprint/plugin/validator/USS/CPJ schemas but no on-chain receipt/anchor schema and no analytics schema; grep kafka|warehouse|snowflake|mintForSignal|EmissionsMinted|AFISignalReceipt across afi-config = 0 schema hits (only afi-config/schemas/validatorConfig.schema.json:79-103 references a mint coordinatorAddress/chainId)
findings.recommendation: Add normative Commitment-plane (mint receipt: signalId/epoch/amounts/beneficiary/contentHash) and at least an interface schema for the analytics plane so external validators can validate on-chain<->off-chain linkage per decentralization rule 4; cross-link Theme C (anchor gap) and AFI_ONCHAIN_ANCHOR_GAP_ANALYSIS.md.

findings.title: Generic normative schemas hard-bind their descriptions to specific org repos, blurring the normative/reference line
findings.evidence: afi-config/schemas/pipeline.schema.json:5 'Schema for signal processing pipeline configurations in afi-reactor'; afi-config/schemas/vault.schema.json:5 'Time-Series Signal Data Vault configurations in afi-infra'; afi-config/schemas/plugin-manifest.schema.json description references 'afi-plugins registry'
findings.recommendation: Rephrase canonical schema descriptions to be stack-neutral (e.g., 'for any conforming orchestrator; afi-reactor is the reference implementation') so an external implementer can tell the rule from the reference deployment.

findings.title: VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC (normative-grade) lives inside the reference repo afi-reactor and its ReplaySession is only conceptual (no schema)
findings.evidence: afi-reactor/docs/VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md:67 'v0.1 is normative for invariants ... and concepts (ReplaySession ...)'; the ValidatorReplaySession is described as '(Conceptual)' and fields 'SHOULD capture' (afi-reactor/docs/VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md ValidatorReplaySession section) with no JSON Schema in afi-config
findings.recommendation: Promote the replay invariants and a concrete ValidatorReplaySession schema into the protocol spec set (afi-docs/specs or afi-config) so external validators can implement replay without reading reactor internals.

findings.title: Dual USS canon (v1 vs v1.1) with no normative supersession; vault/pipeline default to the weaker v1
findings.evidence: afi-config/schemas/usignal/v1/index.schema.json:207-209 provenance required only [timestamp] vs afi-config/schemas/usignal/v1_1/index.schema.json:235-239 required [source,providerId,signalId]; afi-config/schemas/vault.schema.json:43 signalSchema default 'afi.usignal.v1'; afi-config/schemas/pipeline.schema.json:70-71 node signalSchema default 'afi.usignal.v1'
findings.recommendation: State which USS version is binding for replay/attribution (v1.1 carries the audit/reward keys) and update vault/pipeline defaults to afi.usignal.v1.1, or document v1 as legacy-only; otherwise an external implementer may validate against v1 and omit providerId/signalId.

findings.title: Stale doc invariant: overview mandates provenance.timestamp but USS v1.1 makes it optional/legacy
findings.evidence: afi-config/docs/AFI_CONFIG_OVERVIEW.md:122 'All signals must include provenance.timestamp for lineage tracking' vs afi-config/schemas/usignal/v1_1/index.schema.json:235-239 (provenance requires source/providerId/signalId; timestamp is a 'legacy field from v1, prefer ingestedAt')
findings.recommendation: Update the overview to reference USS v1.1's required provenance keys (source/providerId/signalId, ingestedAt) instead of the v1 timestamp; minor doc-drift, defer detailed cataloguing to Theme J.

contradictions.doc_says: Vault finality (T.S.S.D. Vault immutability) is absolute; reputation cannot rewrite history.
contradictions.code_does: The only canonical vault writer overwrites records: upsert sets updatedAt=now and, when a record exists, deleteOne(filter) then insertOne(doc).

contradictions.doc_says: The portable protocol's ingest boundary is the USS v1.1 / CPJ v0.1 dialect (the 'valid entry dialect into the protocol').
contradictions.code_does: The production gateway ingest route accepts a non-USS VaultedSignalRecord shape (identity{signalId,epochId,market,timeframe}) and writes straight to the vault without USS/CPJ validation.

contradictions.doc_says: VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC: same codex version + same config + same data snapshot -> same validator outputs (determinism is normative).
contradictions.code_does: The canonical signal record pins no codex/DAG-topology/scorer version or content hash, and the reference DAG generates execution IDs with Math.random, so a stored record cannot be bound to the exact transforms that produced it.

contradictions.doc_says: AFI_CONFIG_OVERVIEW: all signals must include provenance.timestamp for lineage tracking.
contradictions.code_does: USS v1.1 (the runtime canon) does not require timestamp; it requires source/providerId/signalId and labels timestamp a legacy field.

contradictions.doc_says: AFI_CONFIG_OVERVIEW: afi-reactor and Mongo-backed adapters are reference implementations, 'not mandatory stacks'.
contradictions.code_does: The globally-binding AFI Droid Charter declares afi-reactor 'is the orchestrator' and forbids alternatives, presenting the reference orchestrator as protocol law within the same normative repo.
